get.description:
Health check endpoint.

No authentication required.

List all dining locations with hours.

Returns location info including current open status.

Get menu for a location on a specific date.

Returns menu items grouped by meal period and section.

Get components for a Build-Your-Own item.

Returns available components organized by category (base, protein, topping, etc.)
with nutrition info, allergen info, and selection rules.

Query parameters:
- exclude_allergens: Comma-separated list of allergens to filter (e.g., "wheat,milk,eggs").
  Components containing these allergens will be marked as is_safe=False.
  If not provided, fetches user's allergen_exclusions from their profile.

post.description:
Calculate nutrition for a custom BYO build.

Pass an array of component recipe IDs to get the total nutrition.

Search menu items using semantic similarity.

Uses AI embeddings to find items that match your query, even if the
exact words don't appear in the item name. For example, searching
"something with chicken" will find items like "Grilled Chicken Breast"
and "Chicken Teriyaki".

Query parameters:
- query: What you're looking for (required)
- location_id: Limit to a specific dining hall
- meal_period: Filter by meal (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night)
- date: Date to search (defaults to today)
- area: Filter by area type (residential, campus)
- limit: Maximum results (default 20, max 50)

put.description:
Update user profile.

Changing goal_type, weight, height, age, sex, or activity_level will
trigger automatic recalculation of daily targets (calories, protein, carbs, fat)
unless the user has provided explicit manual overrides.

get.description:
Get today's adjusted targets (with redistribution).

Returns targets adjusted based on week-to-date consumption.
Get weekly or monthly progress stats.

Get pre-computed day plan for the user.

Returns a pre-computed day plan if available and fresh.
If the plan is stale or missing, regenerates it on-demand.

parameters.name: plan_date

schema.description: Date to get plan for (default: today)
schema.title: Plan Date

parameters.description: Date to get plan for (default: today)

post.description:
Log a weight entry and recalculate targets.

When weight is logged:
1. Entry is stored in weight_log table
2. User's current weight is updated
3. Daily targets are recalculated based on new weight (unless macros_custom_override is True)
   - Protein recalculates based on new weight
   - Carb/fat ratio is preserved from user preferences

get.description:
Get weight history for the last N weeks.

Returns weight entries in reverse chronological order (newest first).

delete.description:
Reset all learned food preferences.

This deletes all entries from user_food_preferences table.
Dietary restrictions and allergen exclusions (stored in users table) are NOT affected.

post.description:
Set initial taste profile during onboarding.

Converts slider values (-5 to +5) to food keywords that are used
to boost affinity scores for matching menu items during recommendation.

This is part of the cold start system - new users answer these questions
during onboarding, and the keywords help personalize recommendations
before they have any food interaction history.

Args:
    request: TasteProfileRequest with 5 slider values

Returns:
    TasteProfileResponse with extracted keywords and confirmation

Generate a single meal plate.

Returns optimal plate for the given location and meal period,
respecting user's dietary restrictions and preferences.

Rate limit: 10 requests per day per user (rerolls).

Generate a full day meal plan.

Returns optimal meals across available locations for the entire day.

get.description:
Get all food log entries for a specific date.

Returns:
- entries: List of logged meals with computed macros
- totals: Consumed, remaining, and target macros

post.description:
Log a meal.

Automatically updates preferences if source is "recommendation".

Explicit "love this" signal for a recipe.

Significantly boosts affinity score (+0.35).
Block a recipe (hard exclusion, never recommend).

Analyze a food photo using AI to identify items and estimate nutrition.

This endpoint:
1. Validates the uploaded image
2. Stores it in Supabase storage for reference
3. Sends it to the Food AI service for recognition
4. Returns identified foods with confidence scores and macros

The `confidence` score (0-1) indicates how confident the AI is about each
identification. Values below 0.7 should trigger a disclaimer to the user.

Rate limit: 10 requests per day per user.

get.description:
Lookup product nutritional info by barcode using Open Food Facts.

This endpoint:
1. First checks our local `external_foods` table for cached products
2. If not found, queries the Open Food Facts API
3. Caches successful lookups for future requests

Supports UPC-A, UPC-E, EAN-8, and EAN-13 barcodes.

Rate limit: 20 requests per day per user.

post.description:
Log a correction when the AI misidentifies a food item.

This helps improve the CalAI system by:
1. Building a database of common misidentifications
2. Enabling prompt refinement based on real errors
3. Providing data for potential model fine-tuning

The correction is stored with the original photo reference for analysis.

Re-analyze a food photo with user's description when AI fails.

When the initial photo recognition fails or gives wrong results,
users can provide their own description. The AI then:
1. Verifies the description against the image
2. Extracts a searchable food name
3. Looks up nutrition in USDA (most accurate)
4. Falls back to AI estimation if USDA doesn't have it

The response indicates whether nutrition came from USDA or AI.
AI estimates have lower confidence and should show a disclaimer.

Rate limit: Shares the 10/day photo AI limit.

Check if an email belongs to a supported university.

Use this before auth to show users if their university is supported.

Complete user registration after Supabase auth.

This endpoint:
1. Creates or updates the user record in our database
2. Auto-detects and sets university_id from email domain
3. Returns the user profile with university info

get.description: Get current user's profile with university info.
List all active universities (public endpoint for landing page).

delete.description:
Delete user account and all associated data.

This endpoint:
1. Deletes all user data from our database (cascades via FK)
2. Note: Supabase auth user remains but is orphaned (requires admin SDK to fully delete)

get.description:
Search for foods by name using USDA FoodData Central.

Returns foods with nutritional information including:
- Calories
- Protein, carbs, fat
- Fiber, sugar, sodium (when available)

Examples:
- /food/search?q=pasta
- /food/search?q=chicken breast&limit=10

Get detailed food info by ID.

Food IDs are prefixed with their source:
- usda_12345 -> USDA FoodData Central

post.summary: Generate embeddings for new food items
post.description:
Triggers embedding generation for food items that don't have embeddings yet.

    This endpoint is called by the scraper workflow after new menu items are added.
    Embeddings are generated in the background to avoid timeout issues.

    Requires X-Admin-Key header for authentication.

get.description: Check if admin endpoints are configured and accessible.

Verify the scraper is populating data correctly.

    Returns:
    - hours_today_count: Number of locations with hours for today
    - menu_today_count: Number of menu items for today
    - status: "healthy" (data present), "warning" (stale), "critical" (missing)
    - issues: List of specific problems detected

    Use this endpoint to monitor scraper health and set up alerts.

TasteProfileRequest.title: TasteProfileRequest
TasteProfileRequest.description:
Request body for POST /user/onboarding/taste-profile.

Each field is a slider from -5 to +5 representing food preferences.
These map to food keywords that boost affinity for matching menu items.

Examples:
    comfort_food: -5 = burgers, pizza; +5 = curry, pho, sushi
    spice_tolerance: -5 = mild, plain; +5 = spicy, buffalo, sriracha
